title: Daily Standup — Day 3
notes: alice: PR #101 in review, addressing iOS layout blocker from bob.
bob: Rate-limiter race condition isolated. Fix branch pushed. Still waiting on alice to re-request review.
carol: SCRUM-101 complete. Batch inference branch in progress.
dave: PR #88 approved 3 days ago — needs someone to click merge.
eve: Added SCRUM-112 (security key rotation, 8 pts) to sprint. Confirmed no scope swap done yet.
decisions: Eve to schedule scope-swap discussion for tomorrow.
blockers: PR #101 blocked on iOS fix (alice owns).
SCRUM-112 added without removing points — sprint is now at risk.

action_items.action: Push iOS layout fix and re-request review from bob.

title: Sprint 42 Review
notes: Demoed: dark mode toggle (alice) — stakeholders approved, shipping to prod.
Demoed: embedding pipeline batch inference (carol) — product loves it, expand scope next sprint.
Demoed: batch job runner refactor (dave) — internal improvement, no visible change.
Not demoed: rate-limiter fix (bob) — deployed silently, covered by CI metrics.
SCRUM-112 security work not complete — carries to Sprint 43.
decisions: Dark mode and embedding pipeline accepted for release.
Batch inference scope expansion added to Sprint 43 backlog.

liked: Async standup format — saved ~3 hours of meeting time.
CI flakiness auto-detection caught recurring issue early.
Embedding pipeline shipped on schedule despite scope pressure.
learned: Scope additions mid-sprint without a formal swap process caused delivery risk.
New team members need repo access pre-provisioned before sprint start.
Approved PRs left unmerged create rebase friction for other branches.
lacked: No merge SLA — PR #88 sat approved for 3 days.
No capacity buffer for urgent (security) tickets.
Onboarding checklist missing for new joiners (frank was blocked day 1–2).
longed_for: Automated Slack nudge when a PR is approved but not merged for >48h.
A security ticket fast-track process that forces a scope-swap trade.

action_items.action: Integrate PTO calendar into capacity planning.
Define security ticket intake + scope-swap process.

title: Sprint 43 Planning
notes: SCRUM-112 security work is hard deadline — must ship Sprint 43.
Frank to be unblocked Day 1 via new onboarding checklist.
No new tickets accepted after Sprint 43 Day 1 without a points trade.
Eve will send capacity sheet with PTO marked before kickoff.
risks: Bob carrying both SCRUM-112 (urgent) and SCRUM-118 (flaky CI) — may need to split.
SCRUM-117 depends on external PTO API access not yet approved.

title: Engineering All-Hands — July 2025
agenda: AI/ML platform update — embedding pipeline milestone
highlights: Embedding pipeline shipped on time — first ML feature in prod.
API key exposure detected by automated secret scanning; rotation in progress (SCRUM-112).
Team expanded to 6 engineers; onboarding process being formalised.
CI stability improved: flaky test detection reduced false-failure noise by ~40%.
decisions: Security posture review to be added as standing agenda item each all-hands.
Onboarding checklist approved as official process from Sprint 43.
Q3 north star: ship AI-assisted code review to all repos by end of quarter.
announcements: Frank Li joins the team — welcome!
Sprint 43 will be a hardening sprint focused on security and tech debt.
All-hands moves to bi-weekly cadence starting August.
